text: Data frame columns: repository: Docker repository name (e.g., "myteam/projectcore") tag: Image tag (e.g., "latest", "v1.0.0") size: Image size (e.g., "2.5GB") created: Creation timestamp Returns empty data frame team images found.

title: List available zzcollab team Docker images — team_images
text: function searches Docker images 'zzcollab.team' label, automatically applied team base images created project initialization. Team images typically named following pattern: teamname/projectnamecore-variant:tag Team images serve foundation individual development environments, ensuring team members work identical package versions system configurations.

title: List available zzcollab team Docker images — team_images
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # List all available team images images <- team_images() if (nrow(images) > 0) {   print(images) } else {   cat("No team images available\n") }  # Check for specific team's images images <- team_images() myteam_images <- images[grepl("myteam", images$repository), ] print(myteam_images) } # }

title: Validate zzcollab configuration files — validate_config
text: Performs comprehensive validation zzcollab configuration files configuration hierarchy, checking syntax errors, invalid values, structural problems. helps ensure configuration valid running zzcollab operations.

title: Validate zzcollab configuration files — validate_config
code: validate_config()

title: Validate zzcollab configuration files — validate_config
text: Logical value indicating whether configuration files valid (TRUE) validation errors found (FALSE). Returns TRUE configuration files exist syntactically correct contain valid values. Missing configuration files considered errors.

title: Validate zzcollab configuration files — validate_config
text: validation process checks multiple aspects configuration files: Syntax Validation: YAML syntax correctness configuration files Proper key-value structure indentation duplicate keys invalid characters Value Validation: Build mode values one : "minimal", "fast", "standard", "comprehensive" Boolean values properly formatted "true" "false" Path values syntactically valid (though may exist) File Structure: Configuration directory permissions accessibility File permissions reading writing Backup file integrity (*.backup files) function particularly useful : Pre-flight checks: Validate configuration important operations Troubleshooting: Identify configuration problems causing failures Setup verification: Confirm configuration files properly structured CI/CD pipelines: Automated validation configuration workflows

title: Validate zzcollab configuration files — validate_config
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Validate configuration before important operations if (validate_config()) {   cat("Configuration is valid, proceeding...\n")   init_project(project_name = "my-study") } else {   cat("Configuration has errors, please fix before proceeding\n")   list_config()  # Show current config for debugging }  # Use in automated workflows validate_config() || stop("Invalid zzcollab configuration")  # Validation after making changes set_config("profile_name", "publishing") if (!validate_config()) {   warning("Configuration may have issues") } } # }

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
code: zzcollab_help(topic = NULL)

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
text: topic Character string specifying help page display. Options include: NULL "general": Main help command-line options (default) "init": Team initialization help "quickstart": Individual researcher quick start guide "workflow": Daily development workflow "troubleshooting": Top 10 common issues solutions "config": Configuration system guide "renv": Package management renv "docker": Docker essentials researchers "cicd": CI/CD GitHub Actions "github": GitHub integration automation "next-steps": Development workflow guidance

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
text: Character vector help text, invisible NULL displayed via pager. help text formatted ANSI colors terminal display.

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
text: function provides access zzcollab's comprehensive help system directly R. help page designed accessible researchers without extensive DevOps knowledge, focusing practical workflows examples. help pages displayed using system's pager (usually 'less') running interactively, allowing easy navigation longer help content.

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Display main help zzcollab_help()  # Get quick start guide for individual researchers zzcollab_help("quickstart")  # Learn about configuration system zzcollab_help("config")  # Troubleshooting common issues zzcollab_help("troubleshooting")  # Docker basics for researchers zzcollab_help("docker") } # }
